criteria.name: Tier 4.20c had zero child sim/readback/fallback failures
state ownership table covers core subsystems
runtime stage plan defines staged gates
no exhaustive per-mechanism bridge mandate
criteria.rule: explicitly avoid default exhaustive hardware matrices
criteria.value: Do not run exhaustive per-mechanism hardware bridge matrices by default.

criteria.name: pre-hardware constrained emulation/mapping gate declared
criteria.rule: constrained-NEST plus sPyNNaker preflight before more hardware
criteria.value: Implement constrained-NEST plus sPyNNaker mapping preflight before any further expensive hardware allocation.

criteria.name: continuous target is not marked implemented

memory_budget.current_bridge_representation: host floating-point state replayed after binned spike readback
memory_budget.custom_runtime_representation: bounded fixed-point or hybrid state per active polyp/readout channel
memory_budget.mitigation: range logs, clipping counters, reference fixed-point unit tests

memory_budget.custom_runtime_representation: timestamped decay or compact recent-activity counters, not full per-ms sweeps
memory_budget.mitigation: lazy timestamp decay, per-neuron approximation first, resource budget before full per-synapse traces
memory_budget.risk: SDRAM/DTCM pressure or per-ms sweep cost
memory_budget.scale_driver: synapse count, horizon length, update frequency

memory_budget.custom_runtime_representation: preallocated fixed slot table with bounded key IDs and confidence
memory_budget.mitigation: fixed max slots, explicit eviction policy, wrong-key/slot-shuffle counters
memory_budget.risk: dynamic memory semantics do not map to chip C
memory_budget.scale_driver: slot count, key width, population modules

memory_budget.current_bridge_representation: host replay buffer and offline consolidation loop
memory_budget.custom_runtime_representation: host-led replay first; chip-assisted replay only after storage budget is explicit
memory_budget.mitigation: keep replay host-led until compact replay representation passes controls
memory_budget.risk: replay storage can dwarf model state
memory_budget.scale_driver: stored events, replay length, event dimensionality

memory_budget.current_bridge_representation: host module tables and router scores
memory_budget.custom_runtime_representation: bounded masks/counters; host may still own high-level module library initially
memory_budget.mitigation: pre-feedback route logs, disabled/shuffled route controls, compact route counters
memory_budget.scale_driver: module count, active routes, context count

memory_budget.current_bridge_representation: frequent full traces and CSV export
memory_budget.custom_runtime_representation: compact counters by default plus debug trace windows
memory_budget.mitigation: two modes: proof/debug full trace and scale compact-summary mode

parity_contract.failure_interpretation: software mechanism is not yet hardware-compatible enough to justify EBRAINS runtime
parity_contract.required_artifact: constrained-NEST summary, PyNN compliance report, resource/mapping preflight report
parity_contract.tolerance_or_rule: same task/stream contracts pass without unsupported PyNN features, dynamic graph mutation, unbounded host state, or future-information leakage

parity_contract.failure_interpretation: transport/scheduling problem before learning can be judged
parity_contract.required_artifact: runtime summary CSV plus compact spike counters
parity_contract.tolerance_or_rule: same order of magnitude and no silent zero-spike collapse; exact equality not required

parity_contract.failure_interpretation: credit/plasticity implementation bug or resource-constrained narrowing of claim
parity_contract.metric: delayed_cue tail accuracy and hard-switch qualitative behavior
parity_contract.required_artifact: per-task summary, weight-delta summary, reward/eligibility counters
parity_contract.tolerance_or_rule: delayed_cue remains near 1.0; hard-switch remains above declared transfer threshold or failure is diagnosed

parity_contract.failure_interpretation: keyed state is not represented correctly in custom runtime
parity_contract.required_artifact: slot-event CSV, keyed/ablation summary, compact provenance
parity_contract.tolerance_or_rule: keyed candidate beats wrong-key/slot-shuffle and is not worse than slot-reset; slot telemetry active

parity_contract.failure_interpretation: runtime is leaking information or task contract changed
parity_contract.required_artifact: leakage audit table and phase-separated event log
parity_contract.tolerance_or_rule: control failures remain failures; no future context/reward is visible before decision

parity_contract.failure_interpretation: custom runtime may be correct but not yet useful as a scaling path
parity_contract.required_artifact: runtime breakdown, readback byte/row counts, provenance size
parity_contract.tolerance_or_rule: sim.run calls reduced toward 1 per case and readback volume reduced; exact wall-time speedup must be measured

runtime_stages.claim_if_passed: pre-hardware transfer risk is reduced; final hardware evidence is still required
runtime_stages.fail_gate: NEST only works with capabilities not available on SpiNNaker, PyNN uses unsupported primitives, state/resource estimates exceed bounds, or sPyNNaker mapping fails before science can be tested
runtime_stages.implementation_scope: SpiNNaker-constrained NEST emulation, static PyNN feature compliance, bounded-state/resource checks, and a tiny sPyNNaker map/build/run smoke when a SpiNNaker stack is available
runtime_stages.pass_gate: constrained-NEST parity passes, unsupported PyNN features are absent, no dynamic graph mutation is required, and sPyNNaker can build/map or complete a tiny smoke run in the target environment
runtime_stages.purpose: catch hardware-transfer failures before expensive EBRAINS runs
runtime_stages.required_reference: Tier 4.20c/4.21a hardware-legal PyNN subset and chunked reference traces

runtime_stages.claim_if_passed: continuous hardware scaffold works for CRA-compatible scheduled streams; no learning claim
runtime_stages.fail_gate: requires chunk-loop host intervention, loses scheduled-event timing, or cannot explain missing/extra spikes
runtime_stages.implementation_scope: no plasticity; fixed readout; scheduled sensory/context events; compact spike/decision counters
runtime_stages.pass_gate: real hardware run, no synthetic fallback, <= 1 sim.run per task/variant, compact summary readback, spike totals within predeclared tolerance
runtime_stages.purpose: prove a one-call or near-one-call hardware run can execute scheduled input and return compact summaries
runtime_stages.required_reference: Tier 4.20c delayed_cue/hard_noisy_switching and Tier 4.21a scheduled keyed-memory input trace shape

runtime_stages.claim_if_passed: persistent hardware/hybrid state scaffold exists; still no full on-chip learning claim
runtime_stages.fail_gate: state resets silently, memory slots corrupt, or provenance cannot distinguish state bug from task bug
runtime_stages.implementation_scope: bounded state structs for readout, counters, keyed slots, and optional recent-activity summaries
runtime_stages.pass_gate: state counters/slots persist, reset only on declared reset, and match bridge reference qualitatively
runtime_stages.purpose: prove chip/hybrid state persists across internal decisions without host replay after every chunk
runtime_stages.required_reference: Tier 4.21a keyed slot telemetry and Tier 5.10g keyed-memory software contract

runtime_stages.claim_if_passed: hybrid/native reward-plasticity path works on a minimal CRA capsule
runtime_stages.fail_gate: trace memory exceeds resource budget, fixed-point update diverges, or host still computes all weight updates
runtime_stages.implementation_scope: fixed-point reward/plasticity update, bounded eligibility/recent-activity state, sparse reward command path
runtime_stages.pass_gate: learning metric matches chunked reference qualitatively, weight summaries are bounded, and fixed-point traces pass range/decay checks
runtime_stages.purpose: move the delayed-credit bottleneck out of per-step host replay
runtime_stages.required_reference: Tier 5.4 delayed_lr_0_20, Tier 4.16a/b, Tier 4.20c bridge traces

runtime_stages.claim_if_passed: bounded keyed memory/routing state survives the custom/hybrid runtime
runtime_stages.fail_gate: controls no longer separate or state is implicitly supplied by host metadata after the decision point
runtime_stages.implementation_scope: preallocated slot table, key hashes/IDs, slot confidence, router masks/counters
runtime_stages.pass_gate: keyed candidate keeps bridge-level behavior while slot-reset/shuffle/wrong-key controls still separate
runtime_stages.purpose: move the first stateful v2 mechanism from bridge adapter toward persistent chip/hybrid state
runtime_stages.required_reference: Tier 4.21a keyed-memory bridge pass and Tier 5.10g/5.13c software guardrails

runtime_stages.claim_if_passed: custom/hybrid continuous runtime is a valid replacement for the chunked proof bridge
runtime_stages.fail_gate: behavior only works with chunked host replay, or speed gains vanish under required provenance
runtime_stages.implementation_scope: single or near-single hardware run, compact readback, no per-chunk host learning replay
runtime_stages.pass_gate: accuracy/correlation/recovery/state counters within tolerance; runtime/readback reduction documented; repeatable across seeds
runtime_stages.purpose: prove the custom runtime can replace the chunked bridge reference

state_ownership.audit_signal: manifest records exact command, seed, source revision, and output directory
state_ownership.main_risk: none; moving this on-chip would reduce auditability rather than improve runtime
state_ownership.state_items: task selection, seeds, graph build, run configuration, evidence manifests
state_ownership.why_here: orchestration is not the biological/learning loop and must remain auditable

state_ownership.audit_signal: scheduled-event table plus chip-side event counters
state_ownership.later_target_location: mostly on-chip event/state machine for repeated tasks
state_ownership.main_risk: event timing drift or hidden future/context leakage
state_ownership.state_items: sensory current schedules, context events, reward events, replay windows
state_ownership.tier4_22_target_location: hybrid: host sends compact scheduled event streams, chip consumes continuously
state_ownership.why_here: needed to reduce per-chunk host intervention while preserving exact task contracts

state_ownership.audit_signal: spike count summaries and optional sparse spike samples
state_ownership.main_risk: readback volume can erase runtime gains
state_ownership.state_items: membrane state, refractory state, spike emission
state_ownership.why_here: this is already the hardware substrate

state_ownership.audit_signal: eligibility counters, reward-event counters, weight-delta summaries, fixed-point range logs
state_ownership.later_target_location: on-chip local eligibility/reward update when fixed-point parity passes
state_ownership.main_risk: per-synapse trace memory or per-ms trace sweeps exceed chip resources
state_ownership.state_items: pending horizons, reward timing, eligibility traces, dopamine/reward scalar
state_ownership.tier4_22_target_location: hybrid first: chip stores compact eligibility/recent activity; host may send sparse reward events
state_ownership.why_here: per-step host replay is the main architectural bottleneck

state_ownership.audit_signal: slot-write, slot-hit, slot-evict, wrong-key, and slot-shuffle counters
state_ownership.later_target_location: chip-local fixed-size slot table and routing masks
state_ownership.main_risk: dynamic Python dictionaries do not map directly to chip memory; slot count must be bounded
state_ownership.state_items: context keys, slot values, slot confidence, overwrite policy
state_ownership.tier4_22_target_location: hybrid/on-chip preallocated slots with host-readable counters
state_ownership.why_here: Tier 4.21a proved stateful keyed memory can ride the bridge; next step is persistent local state

state_ownership.audit_signal: online/replay phase separation, replay event IDs, no-feedback-leakage checks
state_ownership.later_target_location: chip-assisted replay after compact memory-store design exists
state_ownership.main_risk: replay can fabricate training exposure unless phase boundaries are explicit
state_ownership.state_items: replay buffer, replay schedule, consolidation writes
state_ownership.tier4_22_target_location: host-led replay window or hybrid scheduled replay, not first on-chip target
state_ownership.why_here: software replay is promoted, but priority weighting is not; replay is expensive and easy to overclaim

state_ownership.audit_signal: pre-feedback route/confidence logs and shuffled/disabled controls
state_ownership.later_target_location: bounded chip-local router/monitor counters where useful
state_ownership.main_risk: high-level mechanism appears to work through host hints rather than hardware state
state_ownership.state_items: module tables, router scores, confidence/error estimates, uncertainty gates
state_ownership.tier4_22_target_location: host/hybrid summary-driven control after lower-level state is stable
state_ownership.why_here: these depend on stable memory/credit state; porting them first would hide lower-level failures

state_ownership.audit_signal: summary schema, provenance hashes, optional debug trace windows
state_ownership.later_target_location: compact summaries by default; full readback only for diagnostics
state_ownership.main_risk: too little readback makes failures uninterpretable; too much readback kills speed
state_ownership.state_items: spike totals, decision summaries, weight/slot/event counters, failure counters
state_ownership.tier4_22_target_location: compact end-of-run summaries plus optional debug windows
state_ownership.why_here: readback volume is a major runtime bottleneck and paper-risk if unaudited

summary.claim_boundary: Design/contract evidence only; not custom-C, native/on-chip, continuous runtime, or speedup evidence.
summary.observed_bridge_runtime_problem: Tier 4.21a took about 58.7 minutes for one seed/four variants; exhaustive bridge matrices are not the target architecture.
recommended_sequence: Use Tier 4.20c and Tier 4.21a as chunked-host reference traces.
Do not run exhaustive per-mechanism hardware bridge matrices by default.
Implement constrained-NEST plus sPyNNaker mapping preflight before any further expensive hardware allocation.
Add 4.22c persistent local state only after 4.22b returns auditable summaries.
Add 4.22e keyed memory/routing state after lower-level state is stable.
Run 4.23 continuous parity against chunked reference before any final hardware claim.

tier: Tier 4.22a - Custom / Hybrid On-Chip Runtime Contract
